title: Coroners
description: Direct activities such as autopsies, pathological and toxicological analyses, and inquests relating to the investigation of deaths occurring within a legal jurisdiction to determine cause of death or to fix responsibility for accidental, violent, or unexplained deaths.

tasks.text: Complete death certificates, including the assignment of cause and manner of death.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform medicolegal examinations and autopsies, conducting preliminary examinations of the body to identify victims, locate signs of trauma, and identify factors that would indicate time of death.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Interview persons present at death scenes to obtain information useful in determining the manner of death.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe and record the positions and conditions of bodies and related evidence.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Provide information concerning the circumstances of death to relatives of the deceased.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove or supervise removal of bodies from death scenes, using the proper equipment and supplies, and arrange for transportation to morgues.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inquire into the cause, manner, and circumstances of human deaths and establish the identities of deceased persons.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe, record, and preserve any objects or personal property related to deaths, including objects such as medication containers and suicide notes.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Complete reports and forms required to finalize cases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Arrange for the next of kin to be notified of deaths.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Locate and document information regarding the next of kin, including their relationship to the deceased and the status of notification attempts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect and document any pertinent medical history information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inventory personal effects recovered from bodies, such as jewelry or wallets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct activities of workers conducting autopsies, performing pathological and toxicological analyses, and preparing documents for permanent records.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate the release of personal effects to authorized persons and facilitate the disposition of unclaimed corpses and personal effects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Witness and certify deaths that are the result of a judicial order.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Testify at inquests, hearings, and court trials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with officials of public health and law enforcement agencies to coordinate interdepartmental activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect wills, burial instructions, and other documentation needed for investigations and for handling of the remains.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record the disposition of minor children, as well as details of arrangements made for their care.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.
